messages.content:
{
  "analysis": "The training data reveals that German legal texts contain person names in specific contexts: judges (preceded by 'Richter'/'Richterin' and titles like 'Mag.', 'Dr.', 'Prof.'), parties in cases (preceded by 'Beschwerdesache' or 'des'), and representatives (preceded by 'vertreten durch'). The main challenge is handling complex academic titles and ensuring the extraction stops before addresses or company names.",
  "strategy": "Use three focused regex rules: one for judges (context: 'Richter' + title + name), one for case parties (context: 'Beschwerdesache'/'des' + name), and one for representatives (context: 'vertreten durch' + name), utilizing lookbehinds to anchor the context without including it in the match.",
  "note": "Every rule includes output_template and output_key. Patterns use lookbehinds to ensure context is present but not captured, and capture groups isolate the name.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "judge_person",
      "description": "Extracts judge names following 'Richter' or 'Richterin' with optional academic titles.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?<=Richter(?:in)?\\s+(?:Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in\\.|RgR\\.|KzlR\\.|LL\\.B\\.|LL\\.M\\.|BEd\\.)?\\s*)[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+(?:LL\\.B|LL\\.M|BEd|Dipl\\.|Kff\\.))?\\b",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 10,
      "reasoning": "Targets judges explicitly mentioned in the court composition section, handling various German academic and professional titles."
    },
    {
      "name": "party_person",
      "description": "Extracts names of parties involved in the case following 'Beschwerdesache' or 'des'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?<=Beschwerdesache(?:\\s+des)?\\s+)[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+(?:LL\\.B|LL\\.M|BEd|Dipl\\.))?\\b",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 9,
      "reasoning": "Captures the primary party name immediately following the case reference, stopping before addresses."
    },
    {
      "name": "representative_person",
      "description": "Extracts names of legal representatives following 'vertreten durch'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?<=vertreten\\s+durch\\s+)[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+(?:Dr\\.|Mag\\.|LL\\.B|LL\\.M|Dipl\\.))?\\b",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 8,
      "reasoning": "Identifies lawyers or representatives introduced by the phrase 'vertreten durch', capturing their full name."
    },
    {
      "name": "herr_person",
      "description": "Extracts names following the honorific 'Herrn' or 'Herr' in narrative sections.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?<=Herr(?:n)?\\s+)[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+(?:MA|LL\\.B|LL\\.M))?\\b",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 7,
      "reasoning": "Catches names mentioned in the narrative text with the title 'Herr', common in legal descriptions of individuals."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": []
}

{
  "analysis": "The training data reveals that German legal texts frequently contain person names preceded by academic or professional titles (e.g., Dr., Mag., Univ.-Prof., Ing.) or appearing in specific legal contexts (e.g., 'Richter', 'Richterin', 'Beschwerdesache'). The primary failure mode to avoid is matching non-person entities like organizations or generic capitalized words; the rules must strictly enforce the presence of a title or a specific legal role context to ensure precision.",
  "strategy": "Implement a set of prioritized regex rules that first capture names with explicit titles (Dr., Mag., etc.) and then capture names following specific legal role indicators (Richter, Richterin, etc.), ensuring all matches are validated against the 'person' type.",
  "note": "Every rule includes output_template and output_key as required.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "Person with Academic/Professional Titles",
      "description": "Captures names preceded by common German academic and professional titles like Dr., Mag., Ing., Univ.-Prof., etc.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Dr\\.(?:in)?|Mag\\.(?:Dr\\.)?|Univ\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Priv\\.-Doz\\.(?:in)?|Hon\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Ing\\.(?:Mag\\.)?|PhD|LL\\.M\\.|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\.|OStR|MR|ÖkR)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 10,
      "reasoning": "High priority rule to catch the most common pattern of named persons in legal documents who hold titles. The pattern handles variations like 'Dr.in', 'Mag. Dr.', and multi-word titles."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after Judicial Role",
      "description": "Captures names appearing immediately after judicial role indicators like 'Richter', 'Richterin', 'Senatsvorsitzende'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:die\\s+Richterin|der\\s+Richter|die\\s+Senatsvorsitzende)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 9,
      "reasoning": "Catches judges whose names might not have a title immediately preceding them in the text structure, relying on the role context."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person in Legal Case Context",
      "description": "Captures names appearing after 'Beschwerdesache' (complaint case) which introduces the party involved.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:in\\s+der\\s+Beschwerdesache|der\\s+Beschwerdesache)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 8,
      "reasoning": "Identifies the primary party in a legal case. The pattern looks for the specific legal phrase introducing the person."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after 'vertreten durch' (represented by)",
      "description": "Captures names of legal representatives following the phrase 'vertreten durch'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:vertreten\\s+durch)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 7,
      "reasoning": "Specifically targets lawyers or representatives mentioned in the text. Note: This might catch company names if not careful, but in this dataset, it consistently precedes a person's name."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after 'Schriftführer' (Court Clerk)",
      "description": "Captures the name of the court clerk mentioned after the title.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Schriftführer)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 6,
      "reasoning": "Catches specific roles like court clerks which appear in the training data."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after 'fachkundiger Laienrichter'",
      "description": "Captures lay judges mentioned with their specific title.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:fachkundiger\\s+Laienrichter|fachkundige\\s+Laienrichterin)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 5,
      "reasoning": "Handles the specific pattern of lay judges found in the training examples."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": []
}

{
  "analysis": "The primary failures stem from the 'Person with Academic/Professional Titles' rule matching incomplete titles (e.g., 'Mag', 'Priv.-Doz. Priv') due to lack of word boundaries and insufficient name length validation, and the 'Person after Judicial Role' rule incorrectly capturing the title 'Mag' instead of the full name. Additionally, many person names appear without titles or specific judicial context, requiring a general name pattern.",
  "strategy": "Tighten the title-based rules with strict word boundaries and minimum name length requirements, fix the judicial role rule to capture the full name span, and add a general rule for standalone capitalized names in legal contexts to improve recall for cases like 'Erna Kaireit' and 'Delila Hofsäβ'.",
  "note": "Every rule includes output_template and output_key. Deleted rules are those replaced by tighter versions.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "Person with Academic/Professional Titles",
      "description": "Captures names preceded by German academic/professional titles, ensuring full title and name are matched with word boundaries.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\b(?:Dr\\.(?:in)?|Mag\\.(?:Dr\\.)?|Univ\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Priv\\.-Doz\\.(?:in)?|Hon\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Ing\\.(?:Mag\\.)?|PhD|LL\\.M\\.|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\.|OStR|MR|ÖkR|KommR|VetR|PhD OMedR)\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+",
      "priority": 10,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Added word boundary \\b at start and required at least two name words (\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+ to prevent partial matches like 'Mag' or 'Priv.-Doz. Priv'. Handles complex titles like 'ÖkR ÖkR' via the title list."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after Judicial Role",
      "description": "Captures the full name (including title) appearing immediately after judicial role indicators.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:die\\s+Richterin|der\\s+Richter|die\\s+Senatsvorsitzende)\\s+(?:Dr\\.(?:in)?|Mag\\.(?:Dr\\.)?|Univ\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Priv\\.-Doz\\.(?:in)?|Hon\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Ing\\.(?:Mag\\.)?|PhD|LL\\.M\\.|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\.|OStR|MR|ÖkR|KommR|VetR|PhD OMedR)?\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Changed capture group to $0 to include the optional title and full name, preventing the rule from stopping at 'Mag'. Added title list to handle cases like 'Mag.a Hedwig Kaszemeikat'."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person in Legal Case Context",
      "description": "Captures names appearing after 'Beschwerdesache' or similar case introductions, ensuring full name is captured.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:in\\s+der\\s+Beschwerdesache|der\\s+Beschwerdesache|in\\s+der\\s+Verwaltungsstrafsache|gegen\\s+Herrn|gegen\\s+Frau|gegen\\s+die)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Added more context phrases ('gegen Herrn', 'gegen Frau') and required at least two name words to avoid partial matches like 'Evelyn' or 'Komm'."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after 'vertreten durch' (represented by)",
      "description": "Captures names of legal representatives following 'vertreten durch', ensuring full name is captured.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:vertreten\\s+durch)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Added requirement for at least two name words to prevent matching partial names or titles like 'Merkur'."
    },
    {
      "name": "Standalone Person Name in Legal Context",
      "description": "Captures standalone capitalized names in legal contexts where no title or specific role precedes them.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:eingetragenen|für\\s+seine\\s+Kinder|für\\s+das\\s+Kind|der\\s+Beschwerdeführerin|der\\s+Beschwerdeführer|der\\s+Beschuldigte|der\\s+Abgabepflichtige|der\\s+Bf\\.)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)",
      "priority": 6,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Addresses false negatives for names like 'Erna Kaireit' and 'Delila Hofsäβ' which appear after context words like 'eingetragenen' or 'für seine Kinder' without titles."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after 'Herrn' or 'Frau'",
      "description": "Captures names preceded by 'Herrn' or 'Frau' in legal texts.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:Herrn|Frau)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)",
      "priority": 5,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Specifically targets cases like 'Herrn Valentina Riehmers' where the title 'Herrn' is used."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": [
    "Person with Academic/Professional Titles",
    "Person after Judicial Role",
    "Person in Legal Case Context",
    "Person after 'vertreten durch' (represented by)"
  ]
}

{
  "analysis": "The current rules fail to capture full names with repeated titles (e.g., 'Priv.-Doz. Priv.-Doz.'), miss names in specific contexts like 'Gattin' or 'Kinder', and incorrectly match partial strings or company names due to overly broad patterns. The 'Person after Judicial Role' rule incorrectly includes the role itself ('die Richterin').",
  "strategy": "Tighten the academic title rule to handle repeated titles and optional 'in' suffixes, add specific rules for 'Gattin' and 'Kinder' contexts, fix the judicial role rule to exclude the role text, and add a rule for standalone names in case contexts to improve recall without sacrificing precision.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "Person with Academic/Professional Titles",
      "description": "Captures names preceded by German academic/professional titles, handling repeated titles and optional 'in' suffixes.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\b(?:Dr\\.(?:in)?|Mag\\.(?:Dr\\.)?|Univ\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Priv\\.-Doz\\.(?:in)?|Hon\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Ing\\.(?:Mag\\.)?|PhD|LL\\.M\\.|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\.|OStR|MR|ÖkR|KommR|VetR|PhD OMedR)(?:\\s+(?:Dr\\.(?:in)?|Mag\\.(?:Dr\\.)?|Univ\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Priv\\.-Doz\\.(?:in)?|Hon\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Ing\\.(?:Mag\\.)?|PhD|LL\\.M\\.|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\.|OStR|MR|ÖkR|KommR|VetR|PhD OMedR))?\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+",
      "priority": 10,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Fixes partial matches by allowing optional repetition of titles and ensuring the full name is captured."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after Judicial Role",
      "description": "Captures the full name (including title) appearing immediately after judicial role indicators, excluding the role text.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:die\\s+Richterin|der\\s+Richter|die\\s+Senatsvorsitzende)\\s+(?:Dr\\.(?:in)?|Mag\\.(?:Dr\\.)?|Univ\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Priv\\.-Doz\\.(?:in)?|Hon\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Ing\\.(?:Mag\\.)?|PhD|LL\\.M\\.|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\.|OStR|MR|ÖkR|KommR|VetR|PhD OMedR)?\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Uses capture group $1 to exclude the preceding role text (e.g., 'die Richterin') which was causing false positives."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person in Legal Case Context",
      "description": "Captures names appearing after 'Beschwerdesache' or similar case introductions, ensuring full name is captured.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:in\\s+der\\s+Beschwerdesache|der\\s+Beschwerdesache|in\\s+der\\s+Verwaltungsstrafsache|gegen\\s+Herrn|gegen\\s+Frau|gegen\\s+die)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Retained as is, but relies on the capture group to ensure only the name is extracted."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after 'vertreten durch' (represented by)",
      "description": "Captures names of legal representatives following 'vertreten durch', ensuring full name is captured.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:vertreten\\s+durch)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Retained, but the capture group ensures only the name is extracted, avoiding company names if they don't start with a capitalized name pattern."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after 'Gattin' or 'Kinder'",
      "description": "Captures names of spouses or children mentioned in legal contexts.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:und\\s+dessen\\s+Gattin|und\\s+deren\\s+Gattin|für\\s+seine\\s+Kinder|für\\s+das\\s+Kind|für\\s+ihre\\s+Tochter|für\\s+seinen\\s+Sohn)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)",
      "priority": 6,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Addresses failures where names like 'Helga Wiebler' (Gattin) or 'Evelyn Boediker' (Kinder) were missed."
    },
    {
      "name": "Standalone Person Name in Legal Context",
      "description": "Captures standalone capitalized names in legal contexts where no title or specific role precedes them.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:eingetragenen|f\u00fcr\\s+seine\\s+Kinder|f\u00fcr\\s+das\\s+Kind|der\\s+Beschwerdef\u00fchrerin|der\\s+Beschwerdef\u00fchrer|der\\s+Beschuldigte|der\\s+Abgabepflichtige|der\\s+Bf\\.)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)",
      "priority": 5,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Retained to catch names like 'Erna Kaireit' after 'eingetragenen'."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after 'Herrn' or 'Frau'",
      "description": "Captures names preceded by 'Herrn' or 'Frau' in legal texts.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:Herrn|Frau)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)",
      "priority": 4,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Addresses cases like 'Herrn Valentin' or 'Frau Elena'."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": []
}

{
  "analysis": "The primary failures stem from the 'Person with Academic/Professional Titles' rule using a hardcoded alternation list that truncates names (e.g., 'Niklas Bu' instead of 'Niklas Bußjann') and the 'Person in Legal Case Context' rule failing to capture names with suffixes (e.g., 'LLB') or when the name is not immediately adjacent to the trigger phrase. Additionally, the 'vertreten durch' rule is too greedy, matching company names.",
  "strategy": "Replace the broken 'Person with Academic/Professional Titles' rule with a generalized pattern that handles repeated titles and optional suffixes without hardcoding names. Tighten the 'Person in Legal Case Context' rule to allow for suffixes and ensure it captures the full name. Add a specific rule for names following 'Gattin' or 'Kinder' that handles suffixes and non-adjacent names, and fix the 'vertreten durch' rule to exclude company names ending in 'GmbH' or similar.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "Person with Academic/Professional Titles",
      "description": "Captures names preceded by German academic/professional titles, handling repeated titles and optional 'in' suffixes, generalized to avoid truncation.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\b(?:Dr\\.(?:in)?|Mag\\.(?:Dr\\.)?|Univ\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Priv\\.-Doz\\.(?:in)?|Hon\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Ing\\.(?:Mag\\.)?|PhD|LL\\.M\\.|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\.|OStR|MR|ÖkR|KommR|VetR|PhD OMedR)(?:\\s+(?:Dr\\.(?:in)?|Mag\\.(?:Dr\\.)?|Univ\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Priv\\.-Doz\\.(?:in)?|Hon\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?|Ing\\.(?:Mag\\.)?|PhD|LL\\.M\\.|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\.|OStR|MR|ÖkR|KommR|VetR|PhD OMedR))?\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)",
      "priority": 10,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Replaces the broken rule with a generalized pattern using character classes for German letters (äöüß) and allowing multiple name parts, ensuring full names like 'Niklas Bußjann' are captured."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person in Legal Case Context",
      "description": "Captures names appearing after 'Beschwerdesache' or similar case introductions, allowing for suffixes and non-adjacent names.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:in\\s+der\\s+Beschwerdesache|der\\s+Beschwerdesache|in\\s+der\\s+Verwaltungsstrafsache|gegen\\s+Herrn|gegen\\s+Frau|gegen\\s+die)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Generalized to support German umlauts and ensures the pattern captures the full name even if there are intervening words or suffixes like 'LLB' (though 'LLB' is a suffix, the name itself is the target). Note: The original rule failed on 'Viola Siebenhüchner, LLB' because the comma broke the simple name pattern; this rule captures the name part before the comma."
    },
    {
      "name": "Person after 'vertreten durch' (represented by)",
      "description": "Captures names of legal representatives following 'vertreten durch', excluding company names.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:vertreten\\s+durch)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)(?!\\s+(?:GmbH|AG|KG|OHG|UG|PartG|Rechtsanwalts|Steuerberatung))",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Adds a negative lookahead to prevent matching company names like 'Merkur Treuhand Steuerberatung Gmb' which were false positives."
